summary: Deterministic proof that the Cautilus Agent surface routes existing claim state to refresh-plan handling and the binary records refresh planning and state-transition facts in packets or helper output.

evidenceScope.targetSurface: existing claim-state refresh branch and deterministic refresh-plan packet boundary
evidenceScope.claimBoundary: Only the docs/contracts/claim-discovery-workflow.md claim that prior JSON selects refresh behavior while refresh plans and state transitions remain recorded in deterministic packets or helper output is marked satisfied.
notClaimed: Every host agent always chooses the refresh branch correctly without reading the skill.
A refresh-plan packet mutates the saved claim map by itself.
LLM-backed review or eval planning is launched during refresh planning.
Every refresh workflow claim in the contract is satisfied by this slice.
The semantic quality of every changed-source decision is proven beyond the focused fixtures.

protectedBehaviors: refresh-plan packets expose baseline and target state, policy, changed sources, claim plan, refresh summary, claim state, and effective scan scope
changed source claims become changed lifecycle entries while unchanged claims carry forward
stale evidence reasons and counts are recorded in packet fields
committed claim-packet drift is recorded without marking source claims changed
discover claims auto-uses an existing output packet as previous state when not run from scratch
refresh-plan output refuses to overwrite saved claim-state proof-plan paths

relatedEvidence.reason: Records the earlier deterministic refresh-plan packet field contract and non-mutation boundary.

decision.reason: The Cautilus Agent skill instructs agents to read or refresh existing claim packets and to use `discover claims --previous ... --refresh-plan` plus `refreshSummary`; parity lint keeps the packaged skill aligned, while runtime and app tests prove refresh-plan packets and prior-state carry-forward behavior are deterministic and non-mutating.
